notes: Widely circulated as a Hendrix quote, but with no documented interview, lyric, or contemporaneous print source.

supporting_evidence.excerpt: The quote is reproduced on countless quote-aggregator sites attributed to Jimi Hendrix without a primary citation.

refuting_evidence.excerpt: No published interview, broadcast, or lyric in the Hendrix archive contains this phrasing.
The earliest verifiable appearance in print predates Hendrix and is unattributed.

text: I'd rather be hated for who I am than loved for who I am not.
notes: Routinely attributed to Cobain online; the closest documented predecessor is André Gide, decades earlier.

supporting_evidence.excerpt: The phrasing surfaces in fan-made tribute posts and image macros attributed to Cobain.

refuting_evidence.excerpt: A nearly identical line appears in André Gide's autobiography 'If It Die...' (1924), long before Cobain.

text: When I was 5 years old, my mother always told me that happiness was the key to life.
notes: The longer version of this anecdote is widely shared as Lennon; the earliest verifiable instances are uncited and from years after his death.

supporting_evidence.excerpt: The quotation is reprinted on motivational sites and social posts attributed to Lennon with no primary source.

refuting_evidence.excerpt: No verifiable interview or letter by Lennon contains this story.

text: Truth is, everybody is going to hurt you. You just gotta find the ones worth suffering for.
notes: Spread virally on Tumblr and Pinterest as a Marley quote; not present in any documented interview or lyric.

supporting_evidence.excerpt: Image macros and tribute posts circulate this phrasing as Marley.

refuting_evidence.excerpt: Marley's recorded interviews and lyric corpus contain no instance of the phrasing.

text: When you're happy, you enjoy the music. When you're sad, you understand the lyrics.
notes: Frequently posted as Frank Ocean; the line is widely attributed elsewhere (often to Frank Sinatra or anonymous) and predates Ocean's career.

supporting_evidence.excerpt: Social posts attribute the quote to Frank Ocean without citation.

refuting_evidence.excerpt: Earlier print and forum appearances of the phrase pre-date Ocean's recording career and attribute it to Frank Sinatra or treat it as unattributed.
